title: mimosa watsonii var. recordii
intro: Mimosa watsonii var. recordii, also known as the sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Mimosa watsonii var. recordii include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
